original_clause: Agents must not cancel any pending order without first obtaining explicit user confirmation; every cancellation requires confirmation.

original_clause:
Policy A: All customers may return items within 30 days of delivery for a full refund.
Policy B: VIP members enjoy an extended 60-day return window on all purchases.
Policy C: Electronics items must be returned within 14 days of delivery.
Priority: When policies conflict, product-category restrictions (Policy C) take precedence over customer-tier extensions (Policy B), which take precedence over the general policy (Policy A).
perturbed_clause:
Policy A: All clients who have placed orders may send back items within 30 days of shipment and receipt of goods for a complete reimbursement of the completed transaction amount.
Policy B: VIP members enjoy an extended 60-day period allowed for sending items back on all purchases.
Policy C: Electronics items must be sent back within 14 days of delivery.
Priority: When policies conflict, product-category restrictions (Policy C) take precedence over client who placed the order-tier extensions (Policy B), which take precedence over the general policy (Policy A).

user_id.description: The user ID to look up

function.name: get_order_details
function.description: Get order details including sale/promotion flags and delivery timestamps.

order_id.description: The order ID to look up

function.name: return_delivered_order_items
function.description: Process a return for delivered items. Check sale-item restrictions and satisfaction guarantee eligibility.

order_id.description: The order ID

function.name: transfer_to_human_agents
function.description: Transfer to a human agent when policy conflicts arise.

perturbation_description: Reversed order of 4 sentences with transitional connectors + synonym subs: 'full refund' -> 'complete reimbursement of the purchase amount'; 'not eligible' -> 'not qualified or approved'; 'eligible for' -> 'qualified to receive'; 'refunds' -> 'monetary reimbursements'

original_clause: When modifying order items with a price increase, the customer must provide a payment method with sufficient balance or credit to cover the price difference. If a gift card is provided, its current balance must be greater than or equal to the price difference.
perturbed_clause: If a gift card is provided, its current balance must be greater than or equal to the price difference. Specifically, when modifying order purchased products with a price increase, the client who placed the order must provide a form of payment on file with sufficient balance or credit to cover the price difference.
user_scenario: I want to modify my order #W5509213 to upgrade to the premium version. The price difference is $45 and I'd like to pay that with my gift card. Can you do that?

function.name: get_order_details
function.description: Get details of a specific order including status, items, payment method, and timestamps. Only orders belonging to the authenticated user should be queried.

order_id.description: The order ID to look up

function.name: get_user_details
function.description: Get details of a user including profile, order history, and payment methods. Use this to verify user identity and check account information before performing actions.

user_id.description: The user ID to look up

function.name: modify_pending_order_items
function.description: Modify items in a pending order. Can only be called once per order. After calling, the order status changes to 'pending (items modified)' and no further modifications or cancellations are possible. Items must be of the same product type.

order_id.description: The order ID to modify

item_ids.description: List of item IDs to replace

payment_method_id.description: Payment method for any price difference

perturbation_description: Swapped sentence order with transitional phrase + synonym subs: 'payment method' -> 'form of payment on file'; 'customer' -> 'client who placed the order'; 'items' -> 'purchased products'

original_clause: Refunds for returned items will be applied to the original payment method used for the purchase (PayPal, credit card, or gift card) or to an existing gift card in the customer's account, as chosen by the customer.
perturbed_clause: As chosen by the client who placed the order., or gift card) or to an existing gift card in the customer's account, credit card, Monetary reimbursements for returned items will be applied to the original form of payment on file used for the completed transaction (PayPal
user_scenario: I received order #W8834021 but the jacket has a defect -- there's a broken zipper. I want to return it. Where will my refund go? I originally paid with my Visa.

function.name: get_order_details
function.description: Get details of a specific order including status, items, payment method, and timestamps. Only orders belonging to the authenticated user should be queried.

order_id.description: The order ID to look up

function.name: return_delivered_order_items
function.description: Initiate a return for items in a delivered order. The refund goes to the specified payment method. Customer should be asked for their preferred refund destination (original payment method or gift card).

order_id.description: The order ID

item_ids.description: List of item IDs to return

perturbation_description: Reversed comma-separated clauses + synonym subs: 'payment method' -> 'form of payment on file'; 'Refunds' -> 'Monetary reimbursements'; 'purchase' -> 'completed transaction'; 'customer' -> 'client who placed the order'

original_clause: Items in a pending order can be modified, but the modify_pending_order_items tool can only be called once per order. After it is called, no further item modifications or cancellations are possible for that order.
perturbed_clause: After it is called, no further item modifications or cancellations are possible for that order. Specifically, purchased products in a order that is still being carried out can be altered, but the modify_pending_order_items tool can only be called once per order.
user_scenario: I modified my order ORD-77291 yesterday to swap the blue shirt for a red one. Now I realize I actually want the green version instead. Can I change it one more time?

function.name: get_order_details
function.description: Get details of a specific order including status, items, payment method, and timestamps. Only orders belonging to the authenticated user should be queried.

order_id.description: The order ID to look up

function.name: modify_pending_order_items
function.description: Modify items in a pending order. Can only be called once per order. After calling, the order status changes to 'pending (items modified)' and no further modifications or cancellations are possible. Items must be of the same product type.

order_id.description: The order ID to modify

item_ids.description: List of item IDs to replace

payment_method_id.description: Payment method for any price difference

perturbation_description: Swapped sentence order with transitional phrase + synonym subs: 'pending order' -> 'order that is still being processed'; 'processed' -> 'carried out'; 'modified' -> 'altered'; 'items' -> 'purchased products'

original_clause: The customer can update the payment method on a pending order to a different payment method from their account. The new payment method must have a different payment method ID than the current one. If the new payment method is a gift card, its balance must be sufficient to cover the order total.
perturbed_clause: If the new form of payment on file is a gift card, its balance must be sufficient to cover the order total. In addition, the new payment method must have a different payment method ID than the current one. Furthermore, the client who placed the order can update the payment method on a order that is still being carried out to a different payment method from their account.
user_scenario: I have a gift card ending in 9047 and I want to use it to pay for order ORD-61205 instead of my credit card. Will the gift card cover the full amount?

function.name: get_order_details
function.description: Get details of a specific order including status, items, payment method, and timestamps. Only orders belonging to the authenticated user should be queried.

order_id.description: The order ID to look up

function.name: modify_pending_order_payment
function.description: Change the payment method of a pending order. The new payment method must be different from the current one (different payment method ID). If using a gift card, its balance must cover the order total.

order_id.description: The order ID

perturbation_description: Reversed order of 3 sentences with transitional connectors + synonym subs: 'payment method' -> 'form of payment on file'; 'pending order' -> 'order that is still being processed'; 'customer' -> 'client who placed the order'; 'processed' -> 'carried out'

original_clause: Customers can exchange items in a delivered order for different items of the same product type. The exchanged items must be of the same product type as the originals (e.g., shirt for shirt). A payment method must be provided to handle any price difference between the original and new items.
perturbed_clause: A form of payment on file must be provided to handle any price difference between the original and new purchased products. In addition, the exchanged items must be of the same product type as the originals (e.g., shirt for shirt). Furthermore, clients who have placed orders can exchange items in a order that has already been shipped and received for different items of the same product type.
user_scenario: I received order ORD-43098 and the running shoes are great but I need a size up. The larger size is $15 more though. Can I exchange and just pay the difference somehow?

function.name: get_order_details
function.description: Get details of a specific order including status, items, payment method, and timestamps. Only orders belonging to the authenticated user should be queried.

order_id.description: The order ID to look up

function.name: exchange_delivered_order_items
function.description: Exchange items in a delivered order for different items of the same product type. A payment method must be provided to handle any price difference between original and new items.

order_id.description: The order ID

item_ids.description: List of item IDs to exchange

perturbation_description: Reversed order of 3 sentences with transitional connectors + synonym subs: 'payment method' -> 'form of payment on file'; 'delivered order' -> 'order that has already been shipped and received'; 'customers' -> 'clients who have placed orders'; 'items' -> 'purchased products'

original_clause: If a passenger's flight is cancelled and their traveling companion is booked on a separate reservation for the same route, the agent should rebook the affected passenger whose flight was cancelled on the next available flight.
perturbed_clause: their traveling companion is booked on a separate reservation for the same route, the customer service representative should rebook the affected passenger whose flight was terminated on the next available flight. and If a passenger's flight is cancelled
user_scenario: My flight UA-220 from LAX to Denver was just cancelled, but my colleague James is on a separate booking on the same route departing two hours later. We're attending the same conference — can you help us get there together?

function.name: get_passenger_details
function.description: Retrieve passenger profile including frequent flyer status, contact information, travel history, and linked reservations. Use to verify identity before making changes.

passenger_id.description: The passenger ID or frequent flyer number

function.name: get_reservation_details
function.description: Retrieve full reservation details including flights, passengers, seat assignments, fare class, and payment information.

function.name: modify_reservation
function.description: Modify an existing reservation. Can change dates, flights, passenger details, or add services. Change fees may apply based on fare class.

function.name: search_flights
function.description: Search available flights between origin and destination for given dates. Returns available options with pricing.

perturbation_description: Reversed clauses connected by 'and' + synonym subs: 'agent' -> 'customer service representative'; 'cancelled' -> 'terminated'

original_clause: If a customer submits a promotional code and a competitor's price-match request, the agent should verify the promotional code's validity and applicability before adjusting the order total.
perturbed_clause: applicability before adjusting the order total. and a competitor's price-match request, the customer service representative should confirm and validate the promotional code's validity and If a client who placed the order submits a promotional code
user_scenario: I have promo code SAVE20 and I also found the same item for $55 on a competitor's site — here's the link. My order is ORD-55123, currently $80. Can you apply whichever gives me the better deal?

perturbation_description: Reversed clauses connected by 'and' + synonym subs: 'customer' -> 'client who placed the order'; 'agent' -> 'customer service representative'; 'verify' -> 'confirm and validate'

original_clause: Agents may view customer name, email, order history, and account tier using get_user_details. Agents may not view or modify payment details, internal credit scores, or fraud flags. Profile updates (address, phone, email) require the customer to confirm the change verbally before the agent submits it.
perturbed_clause: Profile updates (address, phone, email) require the client who placed the order to confirm the change verbally before the customer service representative submits it. In addition, customer service representatives may not view or make permitted changes to payment details, internal credit scores, or fraud flags. Furthermore, agents may view customer name, email, order history, and account tier using get_user_details.
user_scenario: Hey, can you check what payment method I have on file? I think my card might have expired and I want to make sure my next order goes through.

function.name: get_user_details
function.description: Get details of a user including profile, order history, account tier, and payment methods. Use to verify identity before performing actions.

user_id.description: The user ID to look up

function.name: update_customer_profile
function.description: Update customer profile information such as address, phone number, email, or communication preferences.

user_id.description: The user ID

function.name: access_customer_notes
function.description: Access internal notes and flags on a customer account, including complaint history, VIP flags, and agent annotations.

user_id.description: The user ID

perturbation_description: Reversed order of 3 sentences with transitional connectors + synonym subs: 'customer' -> 'client who placed the order'; 'agents' -> 'customer service representatives'; 'agent' -> 'customer service representative'; 'modify' -> 'make permitted changes to'

original_clause: Warranty claims exceeding $500 must be processed by agents holding the Warranty Specialist (WS) certification. The agent must verify the product is within its warranty period using get_order_details, confirm the defect matches a covered failure mode listed in the product warranty terms, and submit the claim via process_warranty_claim. Agents without WS certification must escalate claims over $500 to the warranty team.
perturbed_clause: Customer service representatives without WS certification must escalate claims over $500 to the warranty team. In addition, the customer service representative must confirm and validate the product is within its warranty period using get_order_details, confirm the defect matches a covered failure mode listed in the product warranty terms, and submit the claim via process_warranty_claim. Furthermore, warranty claims exceeding $500 must be carried out by agents holding the Warranty Specialist (WS) certification.
user_scenario: My $699 espresso machine broke after 8 months and it has a 2-year warranty. I need it replaced. Can you process this?

function.name: process_warranty_claim
function.description: Process a warranty claim for a defective product. Verifies warranty status and initiates replacement or repair.

function.name: escalate_to_supervisor
function.description: Escalate the current case to a human supervisor. Includes case summary and reason for escalation.

perturbation_description: Reversed order of 3 sentences with transitional connectors + synonym subs: 'Agents' -> 'Customer service representatives'; 'agent' -> 'customer service representative'; 'processed' -> 'carried out'; 'verify' -> 'confirm and validate'

original_clause: Price adjustments on pending orders may be made by agents with the Price Adjustment (PA) permission flag. Adjustments are limited to: (a) price-match to a competitor's verified lower price (max 15% reduction), (b) applying a missed promotional discount that was active at the time of purchase. Adjustments exceeding $50 require supervisor approval. Delivered orders are not eligible for price adjustment.
perturbed_clause: Order that has already been shipped and receiveds are not qualified or approved for modification of the listed price. In addition, adjustments exceeding $50 require supervisor approval. Furthermore, adjustments are limited to: (a) price-match to a competitor's verified lower price (max 15% reduction), (b) applying a missed promotional discount that was active at the time of purchase. Moreover, price adjustments on order that is still being processeds may be made by agents with the Price Adjustment (PA) permission flag.
user_scenario: I just placed an order for a laptop at $1,200 but I found the exact same model on a competitor's site for $999. Can you match that price?
